title: Pentanema mariae
intro: Pentanema mariae, also known as Centaury, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Pentanema mariae is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as its ability to act as a natural diuretic. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract issues.
taxonomy: Pentanema mariae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a member of the tribe Senecioneae, and the genus Pentanema. The exact classification of Pentanema mariae is Pentanema mariae (H.C. Waters) E. E. H. Martínez, which includes the family Asteraceae and the tribe Senecioneae. This plant is further classified into the subfamily Asteroideae. The classification is a way to organize and categorize the plant according to its characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
